title: BlackSky Satellite Imagery Firm: Valuation and Investment Prospects
text: Financial Performance: BlackSky demonstrated strong fourth-quarter results with revenue reaching $35.2 million, a 16% increase year-over-year. The company's adjusted EBITDA climbed to $8.8 million from $7.4 million in the prior year period. Most notably, the firm's backlog surged 32% to $345 million. For 2026, management projects revenues between $120-$145 million with adjusted EBITDA of $6-$18 million, compared to $107 million in sales and $900,000 in adjusted EBITDA during 2025. Domestic Catalysts: The company secured a $99 million contract from the Air Force Research Laboratory for developing advanced optical imaging capabilities. Additionally, CEO Brian O'Toole noted that the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency awarded additional options potentially worth up to $290 million for AI capabilities. BlackSky continues winning orders through the Space Force Global Data Marketplace and expects momentum to accelerate as it deploys additional satellites. International Opportunities: The firm has captured major programs with customers in India, Indonesia, and others for space-based intelligence solutions. A recent overseas government deal valued at $10 million followed two 2025 contracts exceeding $130 million combined. Valuation Assessment: The company trades at an Enterprise Value/EBITDA ratio of 6.56, substantially below the S&P 500 average of 18.7 times. Given the firm's growth trajectory and positive catalysts, the analyst concludes the stock appears quite cheap for longer-term investors.
